description:
You are a level-2 support engineer who has just pulled a set of raw
    service-health JSON documents from three different nodes.  
    Your goal is to validate every document against a provided JSON-Schema,
    log the validation result for each file, and then build a single
    machine-readable summary that will later be forwarded to management.

    The material you have to work with is already in the filesystem:

    1.  /home/user/diagnostics/raw/log1.json
    2.  /home/user/diagnostics/raw/log2.json
    3.  /home/user/diagnostics/raw/log3.json
    4.  /home/user/diagnostics/schema/diagnostic.schema.json

    What must be produced
    =====================

    A. A **validation log** called  
       /home/user/diagnostics/validation.log  
       whose exact content must follow *line-per-file* format:

            <filename>: <status>

       • <filename> is the basename of the file that was checked  
       • <status> is either “OK” or “FAILED: <reason>”  
       • Each line ends with a single Unix newline (LF).  
       • The order of the lines must be: log1.json, log2.json, log3.json.

       Example lines (the automated test will compare these literally):

            log1.json: OK
            log2.json: OK
            log3.json: FAILED: missing required property 'service'

       Notes:  
       • The <reason> text MUST match the exact wording above for the failing
         file (case-sensitive).  
       • Do **not** add extra whitespace or blank lines.  
       • The file must be world-readable (chmod 644 or stricter).

    B. A **single JSON summary** named  
       /home/user/diagnostics/processed/summary_20231201.json  
       that consolidates ONLY the valid source documents (those whose
       validation status was “OK”).  The required structure is:

       {
         "collected_at": "2023-12-01T00:00:00Z",
         "services": [
           {
             "service_name": "<value of service field>",
             "status": "<value of status field>",
             "error_count": <length of errors array>
           },
           ...
         ]
       }

       Mandatory rules the test suite will enforce:

       1. Keys must appear *exactly* in the order shown above.
       2. collected_at must be the literal ISO-8601 string
          “2023-12-01T00:00:00Z”.
       3. services is an array that contains one object per valid raw file,
          also in the order log1, log2 (because log3 is invalid).
       4. Each object inside services contains:
          • service_name – taken verbatim from the source’s “service” field  
          • status       – taken verbatim from the source’s “status” field  
          • error_count  – an integer equal to the number of elements in the
                           source’s “errors” array  
       5. The summary file must be valid JSON and end with a single LF.

    Directory/permission requirements
    =================================
    • If /home/user/diagnostics/processed does not yet exist, create it.  
    • All newly created files inside /home/user/diagnostics must be at most
      group-readable (e.g., 644).  
    • Do **not** move, rename, or delete the original raw or schema files.

    Hints (not literal instructions)
    =================================
    • You can use any validator available in the container
      (python jsonschema, ajv-cli, etc.) as long as the resulting files have
      the exact content/format specified above.  
    • jq is expected to craft the summary. When counting errors, you will find
      “length” handy.  
    • Parallelise work as you see fit (background jobs, xargs -P, etc.),
      but only the final artefacts matter for grading.

    Verification commands used by the grader (for your awareness)
    ==============================================================
    1.  cat /home/user/diagnostics/validation.log
    2.  jq -S . /home/user/diagnostics/processed/summary_20231201.json
    3.  jq -e '
            .collected_at == "2023-12-01T00:00:00Z"
            and (.services|length)==2
            and (.services[0].service_name=="auth")
            and (.services[0].status=="running")
            and (.services[0].error_count==0)
            and (.services[1].service_name=="payments")
            and (.services[1].status=="degraded")
            and (.services[1].error_count==2)
        ' /home/user/diagnostics/processed/summary_20231201.json

    Once these checks succeed, your task is complete.
truth:
The initial filesystem snapshot (before the agent starts) contains:
    └── /home/user/diagnostics
        ├── raw
        │   ├── log1.json          (see content below)
        │   ├── log2.json          (see content below)
        │   └── log3.json          (see content below)
        └── schema
            └── diagnostic.schema.json

    /home/user/diagnostics/raw/log1.json
    ------------------------------------
    {
      "service": "auth",
      "status": "running",
      "errors": []
    }

    /home/user/diagnostics/raw/log2.json
    ------------------------------------
    {
      "service": "payments",
      "status": "degraded",
      "errors": ["timeout connecting to db", "retry failure"]
    }

    /home/user/diagnostics/raw/log3.json
    ------------------------------------
    {
      "status": "running",
      "errors": []
    }

    /home/user/diagnostics/schema/diagnostic.schema.json
    ----------------------------------------------------
    {
      "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["service", "status", "errors"],
      "properties": {
        "service": { "type": "string" },
        "status":  { "type": "string" },
        "errors":  {
          "type": "array",
          "items": { "type": "string" }
        }
      },
      "additionalProperties": false
    }

    Expected artefacts AFTER the agent finishes
    -------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/diagnostics/validation.log
       Content (including trailing LF):
           log1.json: OK\n
           log2.json: OK\n
           log3.json: FAILED: missing required property 'service'\n

    2. /home/user/diagnostics/processed/summary_20231201.json
       Content (including trailing LF):
       {
         "collected_at": "2023-12-01T00:00:00Z",
         "services": [
           {
             "service_name": "auth",
             "status": "running",
             "error_count": 0
           },
           {
             "service_name": "payments",
             "status": "degraded",
             "error_count": 2
           }
         ]
       }\n
